answers.value:
Canal City Acoustic is a Welland-based acoustic duo featuring Daniel Thompson (vocals, acoustic guitar) and Melissa Carter (vocals, light percussion). Daniel grew up in Niagara and started performing at open mics in high school, later playing rhythm guitar in several local cover bands that focused on classic rock and folk. He has about 12 years of experience playing regularly at coffeehouses, farmers’ markets, and small community festivals across the Niagara region. Melissa has a background in choir and musical theatre and spent much of her teens and early twenties singing in community choirs and church groups, where she developed strong harmony and blend skills. She also has experience leading sing-alongs and family-oriented events, which has helped shape the duo’s interactive, approachable style.

The two met in 2019 at an open mic night in downtown Welland and began collaborating on acoustic arrangements of familiar songs that work well in casual, family-friendly environments like markets and street festivals. Since forming Canal City Acoustic, they have performed regularly at the Welland Farmers’ Market during special event days, at the Canal Days celebrations in nearby Port Colborne (busker stages), and at several local cafés and wineries in the Niagara region, such as Flat Rock Cellars and small independent coffee shops in Welland and St. Catharines.

Their typical set list mixes acoustic versions of classic rock (The Beatles, Fleetwood Mac, Eagles), folk and roots (James Taylor, Joni Mitchell), and more recent pop and indie tunes (Ed Sheeran, Vance Joy, Adele) arranged for two voices and one guitar, with occasional light percussion (shaker, cajón) to keep the rhythm interesting but not overpowering for a morning market atmosphere. They focus on keeping volume and tone appropriate so that shoppers can still chat with vendors while enjoying the music in the background. They are fully self-contained with a compact PA system and are experienced in setting up quickly and adapting to different outdoor and indoor spaces.

In addition to performing, Daniel occasionally teaches beginner guitar to local teens and adults, and Melissa volunteers as a music leader for a children’s reading program, where she incorporates simple songs and call-and-response activities. These experiences have taught them how to read a crowd, adjust their set list on the fly, and maintain a positive, welcoming stage presence. Both are familiar with performing in variable weather conditions and are comfortable playing rain or shine, provided equipment can be kept dry and safe. They understand the expectations of the Jammin’ at the Market series, including bringing their own sound equipment, keeping content family-friendly, and being punctual and reliable for the 10am–11am performance window.
